commit.message:
fix: generate ClientContextFile for all clients, not just first

In multi-client TypeSpec specs, only the first client got a context file
generated, causing <Unresolved Symbol> errors in secondary client classes.
Now iterates over all clients with <For> to generate context files for each.

Fixes 5 E2E failures: client/namespace, client/structure/multi-client,
client/structure/client-operation-group, and azure client-initialization
(default + individually).

commit.message:
Fix: Smart mouse mode re-enables after explicit user disable (#257)

In Smart mode, any keypress unconditionally re-enabled mouse capture —
meaning if a user explicitly toggled mouse off (via Escape), it would
immediately re-enable on the next keypress. The 500ms periodic re-enable
timer had the same problem.

## Changes

**`src/mouse_state.rs`**
- Added `explicitly_disabled_by_user: bool` field to `MouseState`
- `toggle()` now sets/clears this flag — auto-disable (scroll, viewport)
does **not** affect it
- Added `is_explicitly_disabled_by_user()` getter
- `check_reenable_timer()` skips re-enable when flag is set (timer is
still consumed to prevent repeat firings)

**`src/app/mod.rs`**
- Smart mode `on_keypress` now gates the auto re-enable on
`!is_explicitly_disabled_by_user()`
- Escape now toggles mouse capture in Smart mode (previously Simple mode
only)

```rust
// Before: unconditional re-enable on every keypress
if self.settings.mouse_mode == MouseMode::Smart {
    self.mouse_state.enable("smart mode: keypress detected");
    self.mouse_state.cancel_reenable();
}

// After: respects explicit user disable
if self.settings.mouse_mode == MouseMode::Smart {
    if !self.mouse_state.is_explicitly_disabled_by_user() {
        self.mouse_state.enable("smart mode: keypress detected");
    }
    self.mouse_state.cancel_reenable();
}
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Pytorch] Add ignore_raw_node option to GraphPickler (#176939)

Summary:
The Graph Pickler raises "Unexpected raw Node during pickling" when it encounters a raw `torch.fx.Node` during serialization. This occurs in some backward graphs (e.g. IDXCVR model__27_backward_7) where metadata contains Node references that haven't been converted to `_NodePickleData`.

This diff adds an `ignore_raw_node` option to `GraphPickler.Options` that, when enabled, silently pickles unexpected raw Nodes as `None` instead of raising an `AssertionError`. This allows graph serialization to finish even when graphs contain these edge-case references.

Test Plan: buck test fbcode//mtia/accuracy/training/tests:test_graph_serialize; arc lint — no new issues

Reviewed By: aorenste

Differential Revision: D95510476

Pull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/176939
Approved by: https://github.com/aorenste

Distinct single/double quote annotations with nestings-based detection (#253)

Quote-context detection relied on checking the immediately preceding
token, which broke for multi-token quoted content. The single
`IsPartOfQuotedString` annotation made no distinction between `'...'`
and `"..."`, so both were colored identically.

## Changes

- **`TokenAnnotation`**: replaced `IsPartOfQuotedString` with
`IsPartOfSingleQuotedString` and `IsPartOfDoubleQuotedString`
- **`walk()` in `dparser.rs`**: quote context now determined by
`nestings.last()` rather than inspecting the previous token — more
robust and handles all tokens within a quoted region regardless of what
precedes them
- **`Palette`**: added `single_quoted_word()` → yellow,
`double_quoted_word()` → red
- **`token_to_style()`**: routes single-quoted tokens (including the `'`
delimiters) to yellow and double-quoted tokens to red

```rust
// Before: one annotation, both colored the same
IsPartOfQuotedString

// After: distinct per quote type, correctly colored
IsPartOfSingleQuotedString  // yellow
IsPartOfDoubleQuotedString  // red
```

commit.message:
Apply bash acceptance check when accepting fuzzy history entries (#249)

Pressing Enter on a fuzzy history entry blindly ran the command,
bypassing the `will_bash_accept_buffer` check used for normal Enter
presses. An incomplete history entry (e.g. `for i in 1 2 3; do echo $i`
without `done`) would be submitted immediately rather than dropping into
multi-line editing.

## Changes

- **Extract `try_submit_current_buffer`** — pulls the existing
Normal-mode submit logic (single-line check + `will_bash_accept_buffer`
gate) into a shared method
- **Fix fuzzy history Enter** — `FuzzyHistorySearch` Enter now calls
`accept_fuzzy_history_search()` then `try_submit_current_buffer()`
instead of unconditionally setting `ExitState::WithCommand`

```rust
// Before
ContentMode::FuzzyHistorySearch => {
    self.accept_fuzzy_history_search();
    self.mode = AppRunningState::Exiting(ExitState::WithCommand(
        self.buffer.buffer().to_string(),
    ));
}

// After
ContentMode::FuzzyHistorySearch => {
    self.accept_fuzzy_history_search();
    self.try_submit_current_buffer(); // same gate as normal Enter
```

fix: use default overlayfs snapshotter, not erofs (#75)

AKS containerd 2.0.0 doesn't have the erofs snapshotter plugin.
Setting snapshotter=erofs on the runtime handler causes image pull
failures. Use the default overlayfs snapshotter — the shim converts
rootfs to erofs via mkfs.erofs regardless of the host snapshotter.

commit.message:
[Validator] Add validation for createHandleFromBinding (#8205)

Previously, the validator didn't validate the parameters to
createHandleFromBinding. This change adds validation that validates:

* Bind parameter is constant — validates the bind struct parameter is a
constant
* Resource class is valid — validates resourceClass is one of CBuffer,
Sampler, SRV, or UAV
* Index range — validates constant indices fall within [rangeLowerBound,
rangeUpperBound]


Fixes #7019

commit.message:
fix: normalize NIST fetch temp permissions (#71)

The NIST PMI archive stores extracted directories as read-only, which causes cleanup and ad hoc file removal to fail with permission errors. Normalize the temporary extraction tree to user-writable before later file operations and trap cleanup.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
